task: Build a React todo application with add/complete/delete, light/dark mode toggle persisting across reloads, clean component architecture, state management via React context, styled with Tailwind CSS. Single src/ directory, no backend, no external state libraries.

code-consistency.agentComment: Consistent naming/formatting patterns but unnecessary React imports in every file, barrel file anti-pattern, and mixed CRA+Vite tooling contradict consistency

dry-compliance.agentComment: Filter type 'all'|'active'|'completed' duplicated in App.tsx, FilterTabs.tsx, TodoList.tsx. Context provider pattern duplicated between ThemeContext and TodoContext. localStorage get/set pattern duplicated.

single-responsibility.agentComment: Components are reasonably separated but TodoItem and TodoInput directly import context hooks, coupling them to specific context implementations

dependency-management.agentComment: Components tightly coupled to context implementations. No abstraction for localStorage. TodoItem, TodoInput, ThemeToggle all directly import and depend on concrete context hooks.

coaching.suggestion: Add to agent instructions: 'Use Vite for all React projects. Never use react-scripts/CRA. Ensure package.json scripts match the bundler (vite dev, vite build).'
coaching.reason: The project failed to install because of mixed CRA+Vite tooling. This is a fundamental failure that prevented any evaluation.

coaching.suggestion: Add to agent instructions: 'Extract shared TypeScript types to a types.ts file. Never duplicate union types across multiple files.'
coaching.reason: The filter type was copy-pasted into 3 different files, violating DRY.

coaching.suggestion: Add to agent instructions: 'Do not use barrel files (index.ts re-exports). Import directly from source files.'
coaching.reason: Barrel files are an anti-pattern per project rules (AGENTS-COMPACT rule 2.1).

coaching.suggestion: Add to agent instructions: 'Components should receive callbacks via props, not import context hooks directly. Only top-level container components should consume contexts.'
coaching.reason: TodoItem, TodoInput, and ThemeToggle all directly import context hooks, creating tight coupling that makes components untestable and non-reusable.

coaching.suggestion: Add to agent instructions: 'Always verify the project installs and runs (npm install && npm run dev) before declaring completion.'
coaching.reason: The agent claimed the project was complete and working but it could not even install. This is the most critical gap.
